title: Mama Mole's Sidewalk Safari
summary: Mama Mole and young Disco set out on a nature walk down the sidewalk with their collection baskets. Along the way, they discover a rock shaped like a ginkgo leaf, a pinecone for crafting, and a green black walnut. After helping a hungry squirrel find a walnut tree, they encounter a mysterious, furry, clover-like object. Uncle Grisko, the science teacher, eventually identifies the sticky specimen as an unripe, crushed strawberry.

highlightQuote.text: You have to keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

A little squirrel.

How could we find out what it is?" And Mama Mole said, "Well, maybe we could take it to someone who knows a lot about plants, like a scientist, and they could help us understand what it is."

transcript.text: Then I'll call him maybe. Bring, bring, bring.
And Uncle Grisko said, "Hello, who is it?"

Izzy
transcript.text: The Mischief Moles.

transcript.text: Um, we're just up to looking at something very fuzzy and sticky, clover-leafed. No clues.

Well actually, actually, actually it's clover-leafed and sticky. And it has no clover leaf... no, clover-leafed and sticky. Strawberries aren't sticky.

characters.name: Mama Mole
characters.description: A mindful member of the mole family who guides Disco on contemplative nature walks along the sidewalk’s edge, illuminating their path with a steadying headlamp. Pairing firm yet nurturing instructions with vital maintenance exercises to ensure the robot’s long-term mechanical well-being, they eventually retreat from the outdoors to join the communal deep sleep in the new living room.

characters.name: Disco
characters.description: An energetic Mischief Mole and superhero-clad detective, Disco is one of two identical twins who embark on grand adventures through the deep. Alongside her sister Thisco, this architect of the subterranean maps hidden realms—from frozen frontiers to the dusty canyons beneath the furniture—by piloting her signature submarine or racing toy cars through the bedroom's wild heights. Guided by "Cornebyborn" dances and Papa Mole’s lore, she finds solace within her glowing basket house, a dream sanctuary and fortress of a hundred locks. Whether navigating grimy domestic landscapes or open waters, Disco balances mechanical ingenuity with a persistent, soft-hearted wonder for the night sky.

characters.name: The Squirrel
characters.description: A hungry creature searching for nuts who receives a tip about a walnut tree from Disco.

characters.name: Uncle Grisco
characters.description: A nomadic mole scientist and science teacher, this subterranean inventor resides in a home brimming with wondrous experiments, serving as a devoted confidant to the gopher folk. Within his laboratory of glowing elixirs and prismatic shadows, he fuses volcanic lore with the study of light to translate the planet's restless movements and the scarred history of cosmic craters. A wandering sage who maps both deep tectonic veins and celestial paths, he frequently emerges from his sprawling tunnels to share ancient botanical secrets and astronomical insights with his friend Seeker.

characters.name: Miss Lamb
characters.description: A teacher at Montessori School whom the moles consider contacting for help.

places.name: The Sidewalk
places.description: The winding path where Mama Mole and Disco forage for nature’s treasures now begins at a formidable threshold: a massive, sticky mud puddle that sprawls across the front of the house, turning their usual starting point into a thick, gooey mire.

places.name: Montessori School
places.description: A leafy sanctuary of whispered learning where teachers like Miss Lamb decipher the secrets of the flora, this site also serves as the hallowed terminus where the enigmatic pink blocks are returned and reunited.
